title: Bridge Essay: Gabor and Non-Gabor Neural Representations Are Shared between Visual Perception and Mental Imagery.
concept: Mental imagery as real perception — imagination, pareidolia, aphantasia.

body.text: This bridge paper connects the esoteric/philosophical concept of **Imaginal Reconstruction** with mechanistic science from PubMed/MEDLINE.
**Bridge rationale:** Imaginal — mental imagery as real perception, imagination activating sensory cortices, aphantasia.
**Phase context:** Mental imagery as real perception — imagination, pareidolia, aphantasia.

**Gabor and Non-Gabor Neural Representations Are Shared between Visual Perception and Mental Imagery.** — J Cogn Neurosci (2023). DOI: 10.1162/jocn_a_01992. PMID: 37043235. Visual perception and mental imagery have been shown to share a hierarchical topological visual structure of neural representation, despite the existence of dissociation of neural substrate between them in function and structure. However, we have limited knowledge about how the visual hierarchical cortex is involved in visual perception and visual imagery in a unique and shared fashion.
